plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate risk.
Trim SPY to reduce concentration risk and free up capital.
Increase allocation to QQQI for income and stability.
Buy CRDO, a strong setup, for potential gains.
Buy XLV, a defensive sector, to balance the tech focus.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a cash buffer.
conservative.read: This plan targets roughly 1.5% per month. It significantly reduces risk by cutting leverage and diversifying away from tech concentration. Drawdowns should be minimal.

plan: Trim SOXL to reduce leverage exposure.
Trim SPY to bring concentration within limits.
Slightly trim QQQI to reallocate capital.
Add to CRWD to increase exposure to a working tech name.
Buy ANET, a strong setup, for growth potential.
Buy GOOGL, a quality tech name, to diversify holdings.
balanced.read: This plan aims for approximately 2.5% per month. It balances growth with risk reduction by trimming leverage and concentration while adding quality names. Drawdowns are expected to be moderate.

plan: Increase allocation to SOXL to leverage strong tech trends.
Trim SPY to reduce concentration and fund other positions.
Add to CRWD, a high-performing tech name.
Add to SMH to increase exposure to semiconductors.
Buy CRDO, a top scanner setup, for aggressive growth.
Buy GUSH to add a different sector with leverage potential.
aggressive.read: This plan targets around 4% per month, accepting significantly higher risk. It aggressively uses leverage and concentrates in strong performers, including leveraged ETFs. Expect larger potential gains but also much larger drawdowns and leverage decay.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate risk.
Sell volatile sector holdings like SMH and CRWD.
Maximize allocation to QQQI for consistent income generation.
Increase allocation to SPY for broad market exposure and income.
This plan prioritizes stable monthly distributions over growth.
A significant cash buffer will be maintained.
income.read: This plan targets roughly 1% per month, focusing on capital preservation and steady income. It minimizes volatility by exiting leveraged and high-beta positions. NAV can still decline in market downturns, but the focus is on distributions.
